# Say Hi Trip

> แพลตฟอร์มจัดทริปกลุ่มแบบอบอุ่น สำหรับผู้จัดทริปที่ต้องการตั้งทริป เปิดรับจอง เก็บเงิน และส่งลิงก์ให้เพื่อนหรือลูกค้าได้ในที่เดียว
> Domain: sayhitrip.com | Tagline: ส่ง Trip ให้ทุกทริป

## What this project does

Say Hi Trip is a **group trip organizer platform** — NOT a trip marketplace. Organizers publish their own trips and share private links directly with their own audience. The platform handles bookings, payments, and status tracking end-to-end.

Three user roles:

- **Organizer** — Creates/manages trips, sets pricing and capacity, manages bookings, tracks payments
- **Booker (public)** — Views a shared trip link, reserves seats, pays via QR/bank transfer slip or Stripe card
- **Admin** — Full platform management (users, trips, bookings)

## Tech stack

- **Framework**: Next.js 16 App Router (TypeScript, React 19)
- **Database**: PostgreSQL via Prisma ORM (Supabase recommended, Docker supported)
- **Auth**: NextAuth (Auth.js) v5 beta — email/password sessions
- **Payments**: Stripe (checkout sessions) + EasySlip (Thai bank transfer slip verification)
- **Storage**: Supabase Storage for trip images (optional)
- **Email**: SMTP (password reset)
- **Deployment**: Vercel (recommended), Docker

## Public routes (no login required)

- `GET /` — Marketing landing page
- `GET /register` — Organizer sign-up
- `GET /trips?o={organizerId}` — Public trip listing for a specific organizer
- `GET /o/{shareCode}` — Short link → redirects to organizer trip listing (`/trips?o=…`)
- `GET /o/{shareCode}/profile` — Organizer public profile (bio, social links)
- `GET /trips/{id}` — Trip detail (title, description, dates, price, capacity, gallery, meet point, itinerary, booking CTA)
- `GET /t/{shareCode}` — Short trip link → redirects to `/trips/{id}`
- `GET /trips/{id}/book` — Booking form (name, email, phone, seat selection, payment method)
- `GET /bookings/{token}` — Booking status page (for bookers)

## Authenticated routes

- `GET /organizer` — Organizer dashboard (trip list, booking management, payment tracking)
- `GET /organizer/trips/new` — Create new trip
- `GET /organizer/trips/{id}/edit` — Edit trip
- `GET /organizer/profile` — Organizer public profile settings
- `GET /organizer/payments` — Payment/payout overview
- `GET /admin` — Admin panel (all users, all trips, all bookings)

## Key environment variables

- `DATABASE_URL` — PostgreSQL connection string (pooled)
- `DIRECT_URL` — PostgreSQL direct connection (for Prisma migrations)
- `AUTH_SECRET` — NextAuth secret (generate with `openssl rand -base64 32`)
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` — Public URL e.g. `https://sayhitrip.com`
- `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`, `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` — Stripe payments
- `EASYSLIP_API_KEY` — Thai bank transfer slip verification
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` — Optional Supabase auth/storage

## Project documentation

- Brand & CI: docs/brand-ci-guideline.md
- Vercel deployment: docs/deployment-vercel.md
- Supabase database setup: docs/database-supabase.md
- Demo accounts for testing: docs/demo-accounts.md

## Source layout

```
src/
  app/
    (site)/         — Public-facing pages (layout uses PublicShell)
      page.tsx      — Landing page
      login/        — Auth pages
      register/
      trips/[id]/   — Trip detail (public)
      o/[shareCode]/ — Organizer short link
      t/[shareCode]/ — Trip short link
    organizer/      — Organizer dashboard (auth required)
    admin/          — Admin panel (auth required)
    api/            — API routes (auth, checkout, stripe webhook, etc.)
  components/       — Shared UI components
  lib/              — Domain logic (trips, bookings, payments, mail, etc.)
  utils/supabase/   — Supabase SSR helpers
```
